message: Jake
Kristy
Günter
I visited the psychiatrist, but he couldn't meet me because he was too busy.
I had some free time, so I thought I'd relax.
I’m glad I came at just the right time.
You seem like a person who lives just to disturb me.
I was thinking of preparing a report for the conference.

Getting a room right in the heart of Manhattan Island seems to indicate his business is doing well, but
I wish he’d pay a bit more attention to the decor. It’s no different from a police interrogation room.
There’s no one here but me and
According to him, there’s no need for assistants or nurses.
Every time I see it, it’s hard to describe this space as anything but bleak. A French person would escape in three days, and an Italian in five minutes.
It seems he’s not the type of person to warmly welcome an unexpected visitor like me; he’s making an obvious grimace.
As for me,
I’m not shameless enough to easily ignore that grimace. I should take care of my business and leave.
Does he have secrets he doesn’t want me to find out, or is he just a nuisance?
He’s staring at me with a grimace.
Looks like he’s pretty irritated. He usually appears nervous, but this makes him seem not much different from his patients, doesn't it?
Are we talking about Miss Dern again?
There's no other reason for you to be here.
That’s true.
Since I started counseling Miss Dern, it feels like I’ve got a plague spirit following me.
I’d like to prepare materials for the conference, so could you please leave soon?
Conference? Are you going to present something?
It’s a place to discuss various cases.
I am very busy, you know.
What about you?
I met him once since then, and he does seem to have improved quite a bit. Maybe it was just my imagination.
Right?
However, I still don’t really understand. It’s possible that that is her other personality.
Other personality? Are you saying she’s a so-called multiple personality?
I’m not making that determination. I’m just saying there are many possibilities. So, I think we shouldn’t feel at ease just yet.
But,
even if it seems at first glance like she has returned to normal, her tendency to avoid contact with others still remains.
Actually, today,
I got an invitation to dinner.
Oh, that’s progress! Seeking out contact with people on her own is,
quite a good trend.
That’s great. It seems like she’s really
okay now, doesn’t it?
Well,
I can’t say for sure yet. For now, I would say her recovery is progressing smoothly.
For now, I think it’s okay,
but we still don’t know.
I guess, looking back, I've spent my life exploring people's hearts.
Haven't you gotten married?
I can’t get that close to people. I tend to overthink things if something happens. I’m not good at socializing, you know.
I think I will spend most of my life studying the human heart.
That's boring, isn't it? Shouldn't you also experience more ordinary happiness?
I don't need such things. Moreover, exploring the human heart brings me unparalleled joy and happiness.
I want to get back to work soon, though.
is recovering smoothly.
If it is acknowledged, then there won’t be anything more to say.
The symptoms of
seem to be changing positively when seen by . If it keeps recovering like this, there won’t be any problem.
Still looks bothered, but has become much more approachable compared to before.
This person might actually be quite special, or could even be called an eccentric.
For this person, living a typical life might be boring.
I’d prefer the interruptions to be kept to a minimum.
※※※ This is a bug ※※※
